adDomains.note: Comprehensive ad/tracking blocklist for a general-purpose Chrome MV3 declarativeNetRequest ad blocker. ~330 entries (registrable domains plus a handful of well-known ad-only subdomains) covering every requested category: Google ad stack (excluding google.com/youtube.com/gstatic.com and deliberately excluding googletagmanager.com per instructions), SSPs/DSPs/exchanges (AppNexus/adnxs, Rubicon, PubMatic, OpenX, Criteo, Index Exchange, Smart, 33Across, Sharethrough, GumGum, Yieldmo, DistrictM, Sovrn/Lijit, Adform, Teads, SpotX, ContextWeb, BidSwitch, TheTradeDesk/adsrvr, TripleLift/3lift, Media.net, Amazon adsystem), native/content ads (Taboola, Outbrain, Revcontent, MGID, Content.ad, Zergnet), pure trackers (Comscore/scorecardresearch, Quantcast, Hotjar, Mixpanel, Segment, Branch, Chartbeat, FB pixel connect.facebook.net, bat.bing.com, Twitter/X ads, Snap pixel, TikTok analytics, Yandex Metrica, Nielsen/imrworldwide), header-bidding/CMP/anti-adblock (htlbid, prebid, AdLightning, Confiant), and pop/redirect networks (PopAds, PopCash, PropellerAds, AdCash, HilltopAds, ExoClick, JuicyAds, TrafficJunky). I expanded well beyond the seed list with major DMPs/data brokers (BlueKai, Krux/krxd, Adobe demdex/omtrdc/everesttech, LiveRamp/rlcdn, Lotame/crwdcntrl, ID5, IntentIQ), verification vendors (Moat, DoubleVerify, IAS/adsafeprotected), mobile ad SDKs (AdColony, AppLovin, Unity Ads, Vungle, Chartboost, InMobi, ironSource, Fyber, Mintegral), and more SSPs/pop networks to exceed the 150+ target. I deliberately EXCLUDED dual-use/content domains (google.com, youtube.com, gstatic.com, fonts.googleapis.com, cdnjs, jsdelivr, cloudflare, recaptcha, stripe, paypal, gravatar) and googletagmanager.com as instructed. CAVEATS to review before shipping: (1) connect.facebook.net and the facebook pixel also power FB social login/SDK on some sites — blocking can break "Login with Facebook"; (2) t.co is Twitter's link wrapper/click tracker but is sometimes used for legit redirects; (3) app.link (Branch) deep-links can affect some apps' web fallback; (4) a few entries are subdomain paths (yandex.ru/metrika, unity3d.com/services/ads) that DNR matches by host only — strip the path and rely on the host or use urlFilter rules; (5) one entry has a stray leading space (' servedbyadbutler.com') and a couple are near-duplicate subdomains of already-listed registrable domains — dedupe/normalize to registrable domains before generating DNR rules. Also note 'consumimosrenta.com' and similar are known malvertising/pop hosts that rotate frequently, so treat the pop-network section as needing periodic refresh from a maintained list (EasyList/EasyPrivacy) rather than static.

selectors.note: Google AdSense renders ads into <ins class="adsbygoogle">. The ins element is semantically rare and this exact class+tag combo is exclusively the AdSense ad slot.
Exact 'adsbygoogle' class token is the canonical Google AdSense slot marker; no legitimate non-ad use of this exact token exists.
Google Publisher Tag (GPT) creates ad iframes with id beginning 'google_ads_iframe'. Anchored prefix match, exclusively ad-related.
AdSense ad iframes get ids like 'aswift_0', 'aswift_1'. The 'aswift_' prefix is unique to Google ad rendering.
GPT/AdSense ad iframes carry name attributes starting 'google_ads'. Anchored prefix, ad-exclusive.
AdSense iframe name attribute mirrors the 'aswift_' id prefix; unique to Google ads.
Standard Google Publisher Tag ad slot container id pattern 'div-gpt-ad-...'. Universally used convention for GPT ad divs.
Google ad container ids prefixed 'google_ads_' (e.g. google_ads_div). Anchored, ad-exclusive prefix.
DoubleClick For Publishers (now GAM) ad slot ids commonly prefixed 'dfp-'. Anchored prefix tied to ad serving.
Underscore variant of the DFP ad slot id prefix; same ad-serving origin.
Alternate Google Publisher Tag slot id prefix without the 'div-' lead; ad-exclusive.
AdSense attribute carrying the publisher's ca-pub client id; present only on AdSense ad units.
AdSense attribute carrying the numeric ad slot id; appears only on ad insertion elements.
AdSense in-feed/in-article layout attribute; exclusive to AdSense ad units.
AdSense attribute declaring ad shape (auto/rectangle/etc.); only on AdSense ad slots.
Common GAM/header-bidding attribute naming the ad unit path; ad-serving specific.
Hyphenated variant of the ad unit path attribute used by ad-serving wrappers; ad-specific.
GAM ad unit path attribute used to define the slot; exclusively ad-serving.
Google ad server stamps rendered ad slots with this query id attribute; appears only after an ad fills.
Google Active View ad viewability instrumentation attribute; present only on Google ad creatives.
DFP/GAM ad slot marker attribute; ad-serving specific.
Exact (case-insensitive) aria-label 'Advertisement' is an explicit accessibility label publishers attach to ad regions; not a substring match.
Exact case-insensitive 'Advertising' aria-label used to flag ad regions for screen readers; unambiguous.
Exact case-insensitive 'Sponsored' aria-label denotes a paid/ad unit; full-value match avoids false positives.
Exact full-value (not substring) case-insensitive 'Ad' aria-label; only matches elements whose entire label is 'Ad', which publishers use to mark ad slots.
Exact 'ad-slot' class token is a deliberate ad-container naming convention; matching the whole token (not *=ad) keeps it safe.
Exact 'ad-unit' class token names an ad insertion container; whole-token match avoids 'gradient'/'thread' style false positives.
Exact 'ad-container' class token is a purpose-built ad wrapper; whole-token match is safe.
Exact 'ad-wrapper' class token denotes an ad wrapper element; full-token match only.
Exact 'ad-banner' class token names a banner ad container; whole-token match avoids substring traps.
Exact 'advertisement' class token is an explicit ad label; full-token match (not [class*=ad]) is unambiguous.
Exact 'advert' class token is a deliberate ad-container name; whole-token match avoids 'adventure'/'advertise-substring' issues since it must equal the full token.
Exact 'google-ad' class token explicitly names a Google ad block; whole-token, vendor-specific.
Exact 'google-ads' class token names a Google ads block; vendor-specific whole-token match.
Exact 'adsense' class token references Google AdSense ad placement; whole-token, vendor-specific.
Exact 'dfp-ad' class token names a DoubleClick/GAM ad slot; whole-token, ad-serving specific.
Exact 'gpt-ad' class token names a Google Publisher Tag slot; whole-token, ad-specific.
Exact 'sponsored-ad' class token names a paid ad unit; whole-token match is safe.
AMP component element whose sole purpose is rendering ads; the custom element tag is ad-exclusive.
AMP component used for ad/embed network units (e.g. Taboola/Outbrain); ad-exclusive custom element.
doubleclick.net is Google's dedicated ad-serving domain; any iframe sourced from it is an ad.
googlesyndication.com serves AdSense/ad creatives exclusively; iframe from this domain is an ad.
Google's ad delivery host; iframes from it are ad creatives, no false positives.
Amazon's advertising/header-bidding domain; iframes from it are ad units.
AppNexus/Xandr ad exchange domain; iframes sourced here are ad creatives.
Google adservice delivery host (TLD-agnostic match); ad-serving exclusive.
2mdn.net is Google's ad creative/media CDN (DoubleClick); iframes from it are ads.
Integral Ad Science ad verification/serving domain; iframes are ad-related.
Criteo retargeting ad network domain; iframes from it are ad creatives.
PubMatic SSP ad-serving domain; iframes from it are ad units.
Magnite/Rubicon SSP ad domain; iframes sourced here are ads.
Taboola native-ad/sponsored-content network; iframes from it are ad widgets.
Outbrain native-ad recommendation network; iframes from it are ad widgets.
Adform ad-serving platform domain; iframes from it are ad creatives.
Index Exchange (casalemedia) SSP ad domain; iframes are ad units.
Belt-and-suspenders AdSense slot match combining the canonical ins.adsbygoogle with the client-id attribute; maximally specific and safe.
Generic ad-serving attribute declaring the ad type/format on a slot element; present only on ad units.

PRIMARY HOOK. The empty 'data-imp-trk' attribute marks every htlbid/Aditude impression-tracked ad slot on the site. The attribute NAME is stable ('data-imp-trk'); the value is always empty string. Matches all banner, MPU, native, background and sidebar slots across home/results/match pages. Hide these and their parent placement wrapper.

The actual rendered banner creative: an wrapping the ad (day-only/night-only variants). /out2/ is HLTV's outbound ad-click redirector. 15 such creatives across the three pages. Stable: /out2/ prefix never changes.

Every outbound ad-creative link uses the /out2/<base64blob> redirector. Safe stable selector for sold/house banner creatives. Note: confirm no legitimate site links use /out2/ (in samples it was 100% ad creatives).

Aditude (htlbid's ad framework) ad placement container. Carries data-aditude-base-div-id (e.g. '.htlad-HLTV_geo1_mpu_desktop_v1'), data-id-prefix='aditude-placement-<n>', style 'max-height:250px;overflow:hidden'. This is the desktop MPU/rectangle slot. Stable class name.
Stable attribute NAME marking Aditude placements; its value points at the injected .htlad-* ad div. Covers Aditude slots even if the .aditude-placement class were renamed.
Aditude injects/targets divs whose id begins 'aditude-placement-' (e.g. aditude-placement-5545). Stable prefix; trailing number is the placement id.

htlbid/Aditude injects the real GPT ad div with class/id 'htlad-<unit>' (observed: htlad-HLTV_geo1_mpu_desktop_v1). These are created client-side by htlbid.js, so not in raw HTML, but this is the durable hook for the live-rendered ad. 'htlad-' prefix is stable.
Match-page banner wrapper directly after the betting widget. Wraps a data-imp-trk slot with position 'BannerOnIndividualMatchPageDesktopMobileSalesNEW'. Stable class, match pages only.
Top mobile banner wrapper; contains the 'TopMobile' data-imp-trk creative. Stable classes.
Centered ad-slot wrapper (home/results). Wraps .aditude-placement MPU creatives (IndexFirst, ResultTopBannerDesktopMobileSalesNEW). Stable class; safe to hide.
Generic centered ad-placement wrapper used for top banner slots (e.g. mobiletop). Stable class.

Background takeover/skin ad enabler. data-imp-trk slot with value 'Background'. The .bgPadding wrapper plus .bg-enabler / .bg-enabler-child.left / .bg-enabler-child.right paint the page-skin wallpaper ad. Stable classes.
Left/right halves of the background skin/wallpaper takeover ad (data-*='background'). Stable classes.

Left/right sticky sidebar ad rails (sticky-offset). Each holds a 1x1 lazy data-imp-trk slot: 'BelowBackgroundLeftSideStickyWide' / 'BelowBackgroundRightSideStickyWide'. The .secondary-sidebar-container is the stable ad-rail hook.

Lazy/placeholder ad slots: 1x1px divs with data-imp-trk, expanded by htlbid.js at runtime. Covers TopCenter, TopRight, all Index*, Column*, Results*, MatchPage*, Forum/Fantasy slots. Match by the 1px style + data-imp-trk combo.

The randomized creative-wrapper class. In this build it is literally 'BZ4Bl4KkTN' (12 occurrences), but the class string is regenerated per deploy and MUST NOT be hardcoded. Always match via the parent [data-imp-trk] instead. Listed for completeness.

Current-build randomized class wrapping rendered banner creatives (the <a href=/out2/> + img). RANDOMIZED per deploy — do NOT hardcode; here only to document the pattern. Use [data-imp-trk] or a[href^='/out2/'] instead.
Admedo 1x1 ad-impression tracking pixel (display:none, id=153623). Ad-tech tracker; block the domain.
1x1 identity-sync / ad impression pixel (UID-style) inside a 1px absolutely-positioned div on match pages. Ad-tech tracker; block the domain.

Header-bidding loader: https://htlbid.com/v3/hltv.org/htlbid.js — the master ad-stack script (loads Aditude/htlad, GPT, prebid, the /out2/ creatives, slot labels). Blocking this kills most ads. Stable URL.
Google Publisher Tag (GPT) library, preloaded + loaded async. Core Google ad delivery for the htlad-* GPT slots. Stable.
Cadmus contextual/ad-targeting & anti-adblock script (cadmus.script.ac/<token>/script.js). Ad-tech; the path token may rotate but the domain is stable — block by domain.
Lift DSP (admtracker.lib.min.js from pj.l.liftdsp.com; creative JSON from ps.l.liftdsp.com). Demand-side ad platform pulled in via the htlbid stack. Block by domain.
Admedo ad-pixel/cookie-sync endpoint (pixurl). Ad-tech; block by domain.

Cookiebot consent-management (uc.js). Gates ad/tracking consent — NOT an ad itself; included for awareness. Removing it may break the consent flow; generally leave it, block the actual ad domains instead. stable=false flags 'do not blindly remove'.

excludeDomains.why: Twitter/X's URL shortener used for ALL outbound links in tweets and many cross-site links; blocking it breaks legitimate link resolution and redirects, not just ad tracking.

Branch.io deep-linking domain that is widely used as the actual destination for legitimate app/marketing deep links and shortened URLs; blocking breaks real navigation and onboarding flows on many apps/sites.

This is a URL path, not a domain. Network blocklists match hostnames; 'unity3d.com/services/ads' as a domain entry will either no-op or, if naively matched, risk blocking the apex unity3d.com which serves the engine/runtime and real content.

URL path, not a hostname. A domain blocklist cannot match a path; if coerced to the apex yandex.ru it would break Yandex search/maps/services. Use metrika.yandex.ru / mc.yandex.ru instead (already present).
Hosts the Facebook JS SDK used for real functionality (Facebook Login, social plugins, comments, share widgets). Blocking breaks 'Log in with Facebook' on many sites, not just the tracking pixel.
Snapchat static CDN that also serves non-tracking SDK/static assets for Snap Kit features (Login Kit, Bitmoji, Creative Kit). Over-broad; can break Snap login/embeds.

Teads is an in-article video player/SSP; t.teads.tv serves the player runtime in some integrations. Blocking can break legitimate publisher video content, not only ads. Keep teads.tv apex review but drop the runtime host to avoid breaking video.
Entry has a leading space, so it will never match (or match unexpectedly). Malformed list entry must be dropped/fixed (should be 'servedbyadbutler.com').

prebid.org is the open-source project/docs/site domain, not an ad-serving endpoint; real prebid auction traffic does not go to prebid.org. Blocking it only risks breaking the documentation site and provides no ad-blocking benefit.
Not a real ad-delivery endpoint; prebid is a client-side library and bids go to individual SSP bidder domains. Spurious entry with no blocking value and risk of confusion.

This host serves the GA web UI/reporting console (where site owners view analytics) and some GA4 config; it is not the measurement collection endpoint. Blocking the collection host (google-analytics.com / region1.google-analytics.com) is sufficient; blocking analytics.google.com can break the dashboard a user may legitimately visit.

excludeSelectors.why: Anchored prefix 'dfp_' is generic enough to collide with non-ad framework IDs (e.g. data-feed, default panels). Lower confidence than the 'div-gpt-ad' / 'dfp-ad' anchors; risk of hiding non-ad containers. Prefer ad-domain-anchored or 'div-gpt-ad' selectors.

Exact class token '.advert' collides with legitimate content classes such as 'advertorial-free', 'advertise' navigation links, and editorial 'advert' wrappers on some sites; can hide non-ad UI. Keep '.advertisement'/'.ad-banner' which are far more ad-specific.

Case-insensitive exact aria-label 'Ad' is dangerously generic: it matches localized/abbreviated controls and any element literally labeled 'Ad' (e.g. 'Add', truncated labels in some i18n, audio-description 'AD' toggles). High false-positive risk of hiding interactive controls.

Generic token that some CMS/themes use for non-advertising 'additional info' or 'address' containers and layout wrappers; if the matched element is an ancestor it can collapse real page sections. Prefer hiding the inner ad iframe by ad-domain src instead.

Same risk as .ad-container: a wrapper class can be an ancestor larger than the ad slot, and the token is reused by non-ad components in some themes. Hiding the wrapper risks blanking adjacent real content/layout.

HLTV: matches ANY only-child div with both a class and id inside a data-imp-trk parent. This is structural, not ad-specific, and could hide legitimate widgets that happen to sit in an impression-tracked container; too broad.

recommendations: Hide ad iframes ONLY by ad-domain src (iframe[src*="doubleclick.net"], etc.) — these are precise and safe. Avoid hiding generic wrapper classes (.ad-container, .ad-wrapper, .ad-slot) because they are frequently ancestors larger than the actual ad slot and can collapse real layout/content.

Never hide an ancestor element larger than the ad creative. Target the innermost ad node (the ins.adsbygoogle, the [id^="div-gpt-ad"] slot, or the ad-domain iframe) so surrounding article/page content is preserved.

Label-based hiding (aria-label/Advertisement) must target the slot element itself, not a parent section. Drop the bare [aria-label="Ad" i] selector — it is too generic and matches non-ad controls; keep only the explicit 'Advertisement'/'Advertising'/'Sponsored' variants.

Do NOT block dual-purpose hosts that also provide login/SDK/CDN/video functionality: connect.facebook.net (FB Login), app.link (Branch deep links), t.co (X link resolution), sc-static.net (Snap Kit), Teads/FreeWheel/Innovid video runtimes when they back real publisher video. If tracking suppression is desired, do it via cosmetic/pixel rules, not full-host network blocks.

Fix malformed list entries before shipping: ' servedbyadbutler.com' has a leading space (won't match), and 'unity3d.com/services/ads' and 'yandex.ru/metrika' are URL PATHS that a hostname-based blocklist cannot match — they must be expressed as real hostnames or moved to a path/URL-pattern rule, never coerced to the apex (unity3d.com / yandex.ru) which would break real services.

googletagmanager.com is correctly ABSENT from the domain list — keep it that way. GTM can inject legitimate content/A-B-test variants/consent banners; blocking it can blank real page content and break SPAs that gate rendering on GTM. The list also (correctly) avoids region1.google-analytics.com-style breakage, but verify no SPA depends on GA callbacks before enabling GA blocking.

Drop prebid.org / ads.prebid.org — prebid is a client-side library; real auction traffic goes to individual SSP bidder hosts (already covered), so these only risk breaking the open-source docs site with zero ad-blocking benefit.

For HLTV, keep ad-domain and aditude/htlad/htlbid-anchored selectors (these are precise to HLTV's ad stack) but drop the purely structural rule div[data-imp-trk] > div[class][id]:only-child, which keys off generic DOM shape rather than ad identity and could hide legitimate impression-tracked widgets.

Prefer network blocking over cosmetic hiding wherever a precise ad host exists; reserve cosmetic selectors for collapsing the empty slot left behind. This minimizes the chance a too-broad class selector hides editorial content the network layer already neutralized.
